title: Claudia Sheinbaum Presidency — Freshness Refresh Apr 13, 2026
summary: Scheduled freshness refresh — no new breaking events. Dashboard data current as of April 9, 2026.

title: Claudia Sheinbaum Presidency Update — Sun Apr 12, 2026
summary: On the eve of the Universal Health Credential's formal registration launch, Mexico's health system was prepared to begin enrolling citizens at over 2,000 modules nationwide starting April 13. As of mid-April 2026, analysts assessed the status of USMCA review negotiations initiated by Economy Minister Marcelo Ebrard and USTR Jamieson Greer on March 16.

title: Claudia Sheinbaum Presidency Update — Sat Apr 11, 2026
summary: International and Spanish-language media provided extensive coverage on April 11 of Sheinbaum's announced April 18 trip to Barcelona for the Global Progressive Mobilisation (GPM) summit. Trump's 100% Section 232 Pharmaceutical Tariffs Raise Mexico Concerns Ahead of July 31 Implementation.

title: Claudia Sheinbaum Presidency Update — Fri Apr 10, 2026
summary: Plan B Electoral Reform Achieves Constitutional Status After 20 State Congresses Ratify It. Sheinbaum Confirms Interpol Red Notice for Former Michoacán Governor Silvano Aureoles. Sheinbaum Announces First Mexico-Spain Presidential Visit in 8 Years: April 18 Barcelona Progressive Summit. Sheinbaum's April 10 mañanera highlighted a strong set of economic indicators for Mexico.

title: Sheinbaum Presidency Update — Apr 9, 2026
summary: President Sheinbaum unveiled a major energy sovereignty plan on April 8 to develop Mexico's unconventional (shale) natural gas deposits, aiming to cut the country's 75% dependence on U.S. gas imports — she denied the decision was influenced by her same-day meeting with BlackRock CEO Larry Fink. The Sistema Universal de Salud presidential decree was signed, entitling 87 million Mexicans to access any public health facility regardless of formal affiliation. The Chamber of Deputies approved the 'Plan B' electoral reform on April 9, completing passage through both chambers, with implementation required by April 30. The ANTAC transporters' megablockade across 20 states was temporarily paused on April 7 after a provisional agreement with state officials, though the core demands remained unresolved. Mexico's peso held near 17.80 MXN/USD, benefiting from the USMCA exemption on 'Liberation Day 2.0' global tariffs and dollar weakness from a US-Iran ceasefire announcement.

title: Claudia Sheinbaum Presidency Update — Wed Apr 8, 2026
summary: Sheinbaum Unveils Unconventional Natural Gas Plan to Reduce 75% U.S. Gas Dependency. President Sheinbaum formally signed the presidential decree creating the Sistema Universal de Salud on April 8, completing the announcement made the previous day. Mexican Army Rescues Francisco Zapata from Santa Fe Mine After 14-Day Entrapment in Sinaloa. President Sheinbaum met with Larry Fink, CEO of BlackRock — the world's largest asset manager with approximately $11.6 trillion under management — on April 8.

title: Claudia Sheinbaum Presidency Update — Tue Apr 7, 2026
summary: ANTAC Transporters Pause Megablockade After 'Responsible Agreement' with State Officials. Sheinbaum Announces Sistema Universal de Salud Decree: Universal Healthcare Access Across All Public Systems. Measles Outbreak Improving: Daily Cases Down 66% from February Peak.

title: Sheinbaum Presidency Update — Apr 6, 2026
summary: President Sheinbaum's cabinet was reshuffled on April 1: Roberto Velasco Álvarez, 38, replaced Juan Ramón de la Fuente as Foreign Minister, consolidating Economy Secretary Ebrard's influence over trade and foreign policy. On April 6, the ANTAC transporters and farmers' megablockade hit at least 20 states, the largest domestic labor protest of Sheinbaum's presidency, demanding highway security and diesel tax relief. Sheinbaum used her post-Semana Santa mañanera to defend the authenticity of AIFA crowd photos amid social media AI-manipulation claims. The peso strengthened to ~17.83 MXN/USD and Banxico raised its 2026 GDP growth forecast to 1.6%. AIFA recorded 164,525 Semana Santa passengers in 1,233 flights, a new capacity milestone for the airport.

summary: Transporters and Farmers Confirm Indefinite National Highway Megablockade Starting April 6.

title: Claudia Sheinbaum Presidency Update — Thu Apr 2, 2026
summary: On the one-year anniversary of his initial 'Liberation Day' tariff announcement, President Trump imposed a new sweeping 10% universal baseline tariff on imports from all countries.

title: Sheinbaum Presidency Update — Apr 1, 2026
summary: President Sheinbaum launched 'Plan Michoacán for Peace and Justice' — a 57 billion peso, 12-point security and development strategy — following protests in Uruapan and Morelia demanding action against persistent CJNG violence. She defended Mexico's right to supply oil to Cuba despite Trump's tariff threat, confirming Pemex is working to restore crude deliveries cut to ~7,000 bpd. The Buenavista–AIFA suburban train missed its Holy Week inauguration target again, delayed indefinitely pending safety certification ahead of the FIFA World Cup. Sheinbaum unveiled 18 new programs under an expanded Plan México at the National Museum of Anthropology. The U.S. Section 301 tariff probe comment period opened with a Mexican government response due April 15.

Electoral Reform 'Plan B' Emerges After Congress Defeats Sheinbaum's Original Bill. title: Claudia Sheinbaum Presidency Update — Mon Mar 30, 2026
summary: Sheinbaum Defends Mexico's Right to Send Oil to Cuba Despite Trump Tariff Threat. Mexico Files Amicus Curiae in US Courts After 14th Mexican National Dies in ICE Custody in 2026. Government Announces 1.5 Billion Peso Cultural Investment: INBA, INAH, Museum Restorations.

title: Sheinbaum Presidency Update — Mar 29, 2026
summary: President Sheinbaum's approval rating fell to a presidency-low 54% per AtlasIntel/Bloomberg polling, driven by public anxiety over crime and economic uncertainty. Banxico surprised markets with a 25bp rate cut to 6.75% on March 26 despite inflation surging to 4.63% — the peso weakened to ~18.12 MXN/USD. The U.S. Supreme Court voided IEEPA tariffs and ordered ~$166 billion in refunds; the Trump administration substituted a 10% Section 122 tariff and launched new Section 301 probes against Mexico. Sheinbaum delivered historic water rights to Baja California, announced a National Water Law, and unveiled a General Law on femicide prevention. A Pemex refinery explosion at Dos Bocas triggered an oil spill and federal investigation. Estadio Azteca reopened as Estadio Banorte for World Cup 2026 with a Mexico-Portugal 0-0 friendly, while USMCA formal review talks advanced with the July 2026 deadline looming.

title: Claudia Sheinbaum Presidency Update — Sat Mar 28, 2026
summary: Sheinbaum Reaffirms 'Gulf of Mexico' Name Amid Renewed Trump Administration Pressure. The iconic Estadio Azteca — newly renamed Estadio Banorte under a naming-rights deal — reopened to the public after approximately two years of World Cup renovation works.

title: Sheinbaum Presidency Update — Mar 26, 2026
summary: President Claudia Sheinbaum defied U.S. pressure by reaffirming Mexico's Cuban medical worker agreement and backing Michelle Bachelet for UN Secretary-General, signaling an independent foreign policy posture. Mexico formally launched bilateral USMCA review talks with Washington on March 16, with Economy Minister Marcelo Ebrard meeting U.S. Trade Representative Jamieson Greer to negotiate on automotive, steel, and aluminum tariffs. Sheinbaum dispatched a humanitarian aid convoy to Cuba following a nationwide blackout, navigating a careful middle ground between solidarity with Havana and U.S. tariff threats. FIFA World Cup 2026 qualification playoffs opened in Guadalajara and Monterrey on March 26, a direct test of Mexico's security commitments following cartel retaliatory violence after the February killing of CJNG leader El Mencho. Sheinbaum continued pushing back against Trump's calls for U.S. military intervention on Mexican soil, urging Washington instead to address illegal gun exports and domestic drug demand driving cartel violence.

title: Claudia Sheinbaum Presidency Update — Wed Mar 25, 2026
summary: President Claudia Sheinbaum announced at her morning press conference (mañanera) that Mexico will continue to accept Cuban doctors and medical specialists in the country, defying mounting U.S. President Sheinbaum reaffirmed Mexico's support for former Chilean president Michelle Bachelet's candidacy for UN Secretary-General, even as Chile withdrew its own support the previous day.

summary: Sheinbaum Announces General Law on Femicide Prevention, Prosecution, and Reparation. An explosion at the Dos Bocas refinery complex in Tabasco triggered an oil spill affecting coastal and marine areas.

summary: Sheinbaum Announces National Water Law Ending Commercialization, Cracking Down on Overexploitation. Mexico sent a humanitarian aid ship to Cuba to help address the island's acute energy crisis, which has produced widespread blackouts.
